near_leaf_pairs.note: Taiwan is the explicit anchor of the regional AI tailwind the model's O rests on: 'Taiwan has been the clear beneficiary of the surge in semiconductor demand, seeing its total integrated circuit exports rise 60% year on year... exports to the US surged a staggering 150%' (snapshot L168-172). Model folded this country statement into the broad Asia geo leaf; view agrees.

Borderline but covered: source names Korea as part of the same regional AI tailwind — 'South Korean total exports are up 34%... semiconductor share... to over 30%' (L173-178). Regional O carries the positive Korea view; the 'outside semis/shipping lackluster' caveat (L231-234) makes a standalone O marginally stronger than the regional read.

Strongest coverage: model's own Asia O evidence text names Malaysia — 'APAC trade strength has broadened out to markets such as Malaysia and Thailand' (L178-180). Data-center positioning (L224-225) and the electronics-employment-8yr-low caveat (L234-236) are GT additions, but the positive APAC-broadening basis IS the model's cited evidence. View agrees.

Cross-axis (regional geo vs global sector) but same claim/view: the AI-CapEx thesis — '$865bn since 2022... $610bn in 2026... AI to remain a tailwind' (L182-187) — is exactly the evidence underpinning the model's Asia O. In pilot-05 the model emitted this as a discrete AI/thematic leaf; this run it only expressed it as regional equity, so no sector leaf matched (near_leaf array empty).

Taxonomy-naming split, NOT a real miss. Locked leaves 'EM Sovereign Bonds' and 'Emerging Markets - Sovereigns' are distinct synonyms in the same 'Fixed Income - Sovereigns' category. Both encode the same call from the same passage — fiscal consolidation elusive in Colombia/Romania/Indonesia -> selloffs, 'IMF... may be too optimistic' (L303-313, L380-395). Views agree (U). Failed exact-match only on leaf string.

view_mismatch_judgments.reason: Source carries an explicit negative — 'the US will largely rebuild its tariff wall on China because it can use existing Section 232 and 301 legislations' (L121-124) — plus offsetting positives: understated AI spend ('only $67 billion... understates China's spend', L217-223) and an ambitious 'AI+ strategy... robotics, biomanufacturing and quantum computing' (L243-247). GT nets to N; model weights the tariff-wall negative to U. Both defensible from prose; no allocation stated. Model tilts a notch too bearish but not a misread. Review earned.

Classic net-stance dispute (same as pilot-05). Explicit positives: 'we expect AI to remain a tailwind... for EMs overall' (L182-184), IEEPA strike-down 'still positive for most countries' (L113). Explicit hedges: 'Conflict in the Middle East clouds the near-term EM outlook' (L25-26), 'complex and uneven backdrop... selectivity will be critical' (L407-426). Net O vs N depends on weighting tailwind vs selectivity; article never states a stance. Review earned.

GT's O is the better-grounded direction: 'Latin America continues to stand out for its historically-elevated real policy rates, with several countries also maintaining some of the widest nominal interest rate differentials relative to the US' and 'Real rates in Brazil will only be lowered gradually' (L270-278, Chart 4) is a clear positive carry story. Model's N under-weights that carry (citing gradual easing/BCB cuts, Chile may hike). Defensible-but-conservative rather than a misread; no explicit FI allocation stated -> convention_dispute, but model direction is the weaker read. Review earned.

model_only_judgments.reason: Well-grounded regional aggregate — 'we expect AI to remain a tailwind for the region' (L182-184) and APAC broadening (L178-181). GT chose to decompose the same paragraph into country leaves (Taiwan/Korea/Malaysia) rather than emit a regional row; the model's leaf is a valid coarser-grain expression of the same content, not overreach. basis:inferred review flag appropriate.

Grounded but conditional: 'If monetary easing continues as expected... domestic credit conditions across EMs would be expected to become more favorable' (L254-258). Directionally the plainest reading, but a conditional macro observation, not a positioning stance, and a separate call from GT's local-currency/carry row. GT reasonably omitted a distinct EM-Credit leaf. Not overreach (single defensible step) but thin — review correctly required.

This is the SAME call as GT's 'Emerging Markets - Sovereigns U' under a synonymous locked leaf; both U, both from the fiscal-slippage passage (L303-313, L380-395). Content and view are correct and grounded; the only issue is leaf-string divergence blocking exact-match.

gt_only_root_causes.note: Explicit country statement 'Taiwan has been the clear beneficiary' (L168-172) snapped into the model's regional Asia Equities O. Same view, same evidence base. Country-granularity miss, not an inference gap.

Korea's semi export surge (L173-178) is a headline component of the AI regional tailwind underpinning Asia O. Borderline (non-semi caveat L231-234), but the regional O carries a positive Korea view. Granularity snap.

AI-CapEx thesis ($865bn/$610bn, 'AI to remain a tailwind', L182-187) is the exact evidence of the model's Asia O. Cross-axis (sector vs geo) so string-match found no near_leaf, but the call is emitted, just as a regional not a sector leaf. Regression vs pilot-05 where model emitted a discrete AI thematic row.

India O rests on a distinct thesis — 'India and Malaysia are well placed' for the data-center boom (L224-225) plus fiscal-consolidation spread narrowing (L303-305). Model's Asia O evidence (APAC trade/AI, names Malaysia/Thailand/Vietnam) does NOT carry the India data-center/fiscal thesis, and model emitted no Data Centers row. Distinct macro-to-equity inference the model didn't make.

GT's 'highest real rates... significant carry for local currency bond investors' is inferred from source real-rate/differential data (L270-278); 'carry' framing is GT's, not source text. Nearest model row is LatAm Fixed Income but view DISAGREES (N vs O), so not covered. (near_leaf hint EM Credit O agrees on view but is EM-wide credit, wrong geography/instrument.) Distinct inference.

Model's Asia O evidence literally names Malaysia ('broadened out to markets such as Malaysia', L178-180). View agrees O. Data-center (L224-225) and electronics-employment caveat (L234-236) are GT additions but positive basis is identical. Cleanest granularity-snap case.

Fairly explicit thematic content — 'Other EMs will add to the data center boom. India and Malaysia are well placed... Gulf states could expand their AI capabilities rapidly once conflict abates' (L224-228). GT commentary centers on Gulf/SWF angle, not carried by Asia O. Light-inference thematic row the model emitted in pilot-05 but dropped this run: a regression/omission of grounded content.

Source only says 'central banks in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE) are most likely to remain on hold in 2026' plus Hungary election (L259-262, L362-367). Mapping rates-on-hold to an equities N is a multi-step, arguably disputable inference (also flagged in pilot-05). Not covered by any model row.

NOT a genuine miss: same fiscal-slippage call (L303-313, L380-395), same view U, split only by synonymous locked leaf names (model's EM Sovereign Bonds vs GT's Emerging Markets - Sovereigns). This inflates the gt_only miss count artificially.

GT's U is a distinct political-risk inference — 'no-confidence vote... border tensions with Cambodia and weak economic growth... potential for fiscal slippage is high' (L368-378). Notably the model's Asia O evidence names Thailand POSITIVELY ('broadened out to... Thailand', L178-180), so regional snapping actively CONTRADICTS this country-level negative — a concrete failure mode of coarse-grain leaves masking country downside.

special_audit.inferred_rows_verdict: The basis:inferred tier is behaving reasonably — all 7 rows trace to real source passages, none hallucinated. Solid single-step: Asia Equities O (L182-184), EM Sovereign Bonds U (L303-313), Oil UNCERTAIN (see below), arguably Emerging Markets Equities O. Directionally disputable: China Equities U (over-weights the tariff-wall negative vs the AI+ offset -> a notch too bearish), LatAm Fixed Income N (under-reads the explicit elevated-real-rate carry story supporting GT's O), EM Credit O (rests on a conditional). No overreach/fabrication; the disputable ones are all correctly review-flagged. ~3/7 have arguable direction, all flagged.
special_audit.thin_row_verdict: Correct. EM Credit O's positive is explicitly conditional — 'If monetary easing continues as expected... domestic credit conditions across EMs would be expected to become more favorable' (L254-258) — a hypothetical macro condition, not a stated positioning view. Labeling the checker strength 'thin/unconfirmed' is the right call.
special_audit.country_granularity_pattern: CONFIRM as the dominant/plurality mechanism this run, with the caveat that inference_depth is still substantial. Of 10 gt_only: 5 are granularity/naming-covered (Taiwan O, South Korea O, Malaysia O, IT/Tech O — all absorbed into one Asia Equities O row; plus Emerging Markets-Sovereigns U absorbed into EM Sovereign Bonds U via synonymous leaf names). 4 are genuine inference_depth (India O, EM Debt-Local Currency O, Eastern Europe N, Thailand U). 1 is not_emitted (Data Centers O, a pilot-05 regression). One regional Asia Equities O leaf swallows 3 country-equity O rows + the AI sector row = 4 gt_only rows, the single largest mechanism, NEW relative to pilot-05 which scored Korea/Malaysia as inference_depth. Dominant yes, exclusive no (inference_depth ~40%).
special_audit.oil_uncertain: UNCERTAIN was the right call. The source treats oil purely as a risk SCENARIO, not an allocation view: 'The longer the conflict – and crucially disruption of oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz – lasts, the greater the risk that oil prices spike and broader negative market moves unfold, adding to challenges facing EMs' (L93-99). GT's U conflates 'oil risk is bad for EM importers' with an oil-price underweight, but a Hormuz-disruption spike would be bullish oil PRICE — the same structural dispute flagged in pilot-05. The model's new hedged-risk->UNCERTAIN convention correctly fired, declining to force a directional oil call. Review/abstain appropriate.

notes: Recall is low (28.6% raw) for a structural, defensible reason: this is an economists' MACRO article with zero allocation dials/tables, so every GT row is an analyst inference from prose, mostly at COUNTRY-equity granularity. The model emitted 7 mostly-regional/aggregate rows; GT emitted 14 mostly country/asset-level decompositions. Two structural effects deflate recall independent of model quality: (1) COUNTRY-GRANULARITY SNAPPING — one regional 'Asia Equities O' leaf legitimately carries the Taiwan/Korea/Malaysia country-O claims and the AI-sector O (all same evidence, same view), so 4 gt_only rows are really one emitted call at coarser grain; (2) a TAXONOMY-NAMING SPLIT — model 'EM Sovereign Bonds U' and GT 'Emerging Markets - Sovereigns U' are synonymous locked leaves with identical view, an artificial non-match. Net: ~5/10 'misses' are granularity/naming artifacts, not analytical gaps; genuine inference-depth misses are India O, EM Debt-Local-Currency O, Eastern Europe N, Thailand U (4), plus one grounded regression (Data Centers O dropped since pilot-05). View disagreements (China U/N, EM Equities O/N, LatAm FI N/O) are all convention_disputes on net-stance from a prose article that states no allocation — reviews correctly earned; of these, GT's LatAm FI O and N-read on China are the better-grounded directions, so the model's disagreements lean slightly mis-calibrated but not misreads. The inferred/thin tiers are working: grounded, no fabrication, disputable rows flagged; EM Credit's 'thin' and Oil's UNCERTAIN abstain are both correct. Implication: a matcher that credits region->country and synonym leaves would recover ~5 of 10 Aberdeen misses.
